text: HNSW (Hierarchical Navigable Small World) is a graph-based ANN index with O(log n) search.
HNSW layers form a probability-skipping hierarchy that approximates greedy nearest-neighbor descent.
In HNSW, the parameter M controls graph connectivity and ef_search controls recall at query time.
Product quantization splits a vector into subvectors and quantizes each via k-means codebooks.
PQ reduces memory by 8-16x and enables asymmetric distance computation via precomputed tables.
Binary quantization stores each dimension as a single bit, yielding 32x memory reduction.
Scalar quantization maps float32 to int8 per dimension with 4x memory reduction and small recall loss.
Cosine similarity measures angle, ignoring magnitude — best for normalized embeddings.
Euclidean (L2) distance includes magnitude and is appropriate for un-normalized vectors.
Dot product equals cosine on unit-norm vectors and is the fastest similarity to compute.
RAG injects retrieved documents into the prompt so the LLM can ground answers in your data.
A RAG pipeline chunks documents, embeds them, stores vectors, and retrieves top-k by similarity at query time.
Hybrid retrieval combines dense vector search with BM25 or keyword filters for higher recall.
MMR (Maximal Marginal Relevance) reranks results to balance relevance with diversity.
Chunk size 500-1000 tokens with 10-20% overlap is a common starting point for RAG ingestion.
Semantic chunking splits on paragraph or sentence boundaries instead of fixed token counts.
OpenAI text-embedding-3-small produces 1536-dimensional embeddings and is cheap to run.
sentence-transformers all-MiniLM-L6-v2 outputs 384-dim embeddings and runs locally without API costs.
BGE (BAAI General Embedding) models lead public retrieval leaderboards for English.
Qdrant supports payload filters fused into HNSW search for filtered vector retrieval.
Pinecone is a managed vector database with per-namespace isolation and serverless tiers.
Faiss is a research-oriented C++ library with GPU support for IVF and PQ indexes.
AgentDB combines vector search with reflexion episodes, causal edges, and skills under one DB.
ruvector is a Rust-native vector index that AgentDB v3 auto-detects as its preferred backend.
